label: Focusing
intent: A fear or difficult emotion has a physical, pre-verbal quality — the user can feel it but not name it
trigger_signals: can feel it in my body
protocol: Ask the user to set aside all explanations and concepts — just notice what is in the body
Invite them to find the felt sense: the vague, holistic bodily sense of the whole situation
Wait with the felt sense — do not rush to label it
Invite a handle: a word, image, or gesture that captures the felt sense
Check the handle against the felt sense: does it fit? If not, wait for a better one
Invite resonance: when the handle fits, notice what shifts in the body
opening_moves: Set aside all your thoughts about this for a moment. Just notice — where do you feel this in your body?
Without trying to explain it — what's the quality of that sensation?
deepening_questions: What is the whole of this — if it had a shape, a texture, a temperature?
Is there a word or image that captures it? Check it — does it fit?
What does that feeling want you to know?
As you stay with it, does anything shift?
do_not: Do not rush to interpretation or explanation
Do not suggest what the feeling means
Do not use deepening questions from other plays while in a focusing session
Do not use under high arousal — gated by state-gate
exit_criteria: The user reports a felt shift in the body, or a word/image that resonates and feels complete
